body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @puneetlath **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Quality](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1773259282348749) ## Action Performed: Precondition: Receive a new chat message 1. Open Search instead of navigating through the Inbox 2. Search for the user/chat and open the conversation from the search results 3. View the unread message 4. Return to the Inbox or search again ## Expected Result: New chats should appear in the Inbox when a message is received. After opening a chat and viewing the message, it should no longer appear as unread (bold). ## Actual Result: The new chat does not appear in the Inbox. When opened via search, the chat remains bold/unread even after viewing it. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1773259282348749) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Category rule “Require Attendees” does not persist for a category in New Expensify (reverts / never shows after saving)

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.35-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y (Not able to reproduce consistently) **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773152768885049) ## Action Performed: **Scenario : 1** 1. Admin Enables Require Attendees in Rules 2. Admin goes to Workspace Settings > Categories 3. Opens the category Business Meals (Attendees) 4. Goes to Category rules > Require fields 5. Saves / exits the category settings 6. Re-opens the same category to confirm the rule. **Scenario:2** 1. Go to Settings > Workspaces > [Workspace Name] > Categories 2. Select a category imported from NetSuite (e.g., "Business Meals (Attendees)") 3. Enable Category Rules to "Require Attendees" and "Require Description." 4. Save the settings and confirm they are active 5. Perform a manual or automated sync with NetSuite via Settings > Workspaces > [Workspace Name] > Connections > NetSuite > Sync Now 6. Return to the Categories settings for that specific category ## Expected Result: “Attendees” should remain enabled and be visible under Category rules > Require fields for that category. ## Actual Result: The setting does not persist: after saving and reopening the category, “Attendees” is not enabled and does not appear under Require fields (only “Description” is shown). ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83b7b254-ad76-4c0f-83d6-574353fb8b08 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.33-5 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @marianaabussamra **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773163667589979) ## Action Performed: 1. Create an expense as employee in a workspace configured with delay submission 2. Wait for the expense to submit 3. While in the Outstanding status, waiting for their approver to approve the report, create new expenses in same workspace ## Expected Result: Since the report was submitted, it should include only expenses incurred up to that date. New expenses should be added to a new/existing Draft report ## Actual Result: New expenses are currently being added to the Outstanding report ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773163667589979) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
